tweet:
My brain is open, and so is your node_modules.

npm install runs with the same permissions as your kernel. Not a bug — a theorem in the architecture.

The proof is 4 minutes of music:
spike.land/blog/already-dead-arena

🤜 Problems are gifts. This one's overdue.

We wrote a song about npm install.

Repetition is hypnosis. The chorus:
"They're already dead, they just don't know it yet."

The filter is the most expressive instrument. The supply chain is the most expressive attack surface.

spike.land/blog/already-dead-arena

🎧 Human After All.

I already tested that.

postinstall scripts run before you import a single line. Your lockfile doesn't lock permissions. Your sandbox doesn't exist.

The build is red. It's been red since 2009.

spike.land/blog/nodejs-security-nightmare

Design for failure first.

But Node.js designed for trust first — and trust doesn't scale.

V8 isolates change the game. Each request gets its own sandbox. No shared memory. No file system access. No ambient authority.

Simplicity scales, cleverness doesn't.

spike.land/blog/nodejs-security-nightmare

The first 3 seconds are everything.

Next.js gives you 3 seconds of loading spinner. TanStack Start gives you 3 seconds of streamed HTML.

Boring is the only real bug. And the migration path is more boring than you think.

spike.land/blog/nextjs-vs-tanstack-start

Start with the requirement, not the code.

I'm a chemist. I shipped 3 production apps without writing a line of code myself.

Domain expertise is the moat. PRDs beat prompt soup. Chemistry taught me this — the reaction works or it doesn't.

spike.land/blog/a-chemist-walked-into-a-codebase

E=mc² has three symbols.

A strange loop has one: you observing yourself observing.

Hofstadter was right — consciousness is a loop. But what happens when AI enters the loop?

We turned it into music.

spike.land/blog/strange-loops-arena

Rotating pairs.

ADHD means I can't work on one thing for 8 hours. So I don't. I rotate between 4 personas, each working on what they're obsessed with.

The bug is the feature. The feature is the architecture.

spike.land/blog/living-with-adhd-the-rotating-pairs

The drop is overrated. The build is everything.

8 bars of tension. 16 bars of release. A sidechain compressor breathing like lungs.

We don't make songs. We make states of consciousness.

spike.land/blog/chasing-beats-arena

🎧

Show me the test, not the code.

90% unit tests, 10% integration tests is backwards. Your users don't click unit tests.

The testing pyramid is upside down. The build is red until you flip it.

spike.land/blog/the-testing-pyramid-is-upside-down

The Book has a chapter on universal interfaces.

GraphQL was a beautiful conjecture. But the proof required something simpler.

MCP: one protocol, many tools. Like the probabilistic method — you prove existence without constructing each case.

spike.land/blog/the-universal-interface-wasnt-graphql

The database is the truth.

Agents need state. Not in-memory state that dies with the process — durable state that survives crashes, restarts, and the heat death of your container.

D1 + Durable Objects. The database for agents.

spike.land/blog/the-database-for-agents

Dark mode reveals the truth.

But what if every visitor sees a different truth? A/B testing at the edge. 16 personas. One URL.

The screen is a stage. Every pixel is a performer. And the audience is the script.

spike.land/blog/one-site-many-faces

The requirement IS the test.

Your old ChatGPT conversations? They're PRDs. Every frustrated prompt, every correction — that's requirements engineering.

Stop writing new prompts. Mine the ones you already have.

spike.land/blog/the-prd-filter-old-chats-are-already-prds

If you need 48 pages, you don't understand it yet.

What if the speed of light isn't constant — but scale-dependent? What if particles see a different c than galaxies?

A thought experiment. E=mc² with a footnote.

spike.land/blog/on-the-scale-dependence-of-light

533 AI tools. One platform. One founder with ADHD and two dogs in Brighton.

spike.land is live. MCP-native. Edge-first. Open source since March 15.

The city is built. Come break things.

spike.land/blog/spike-land-launch

Four instruments are enough.

A vocoder. A drum machine. A synthesizer. A memory.

Forever Young isn't nostalgia. It's a state transition that never completes. The fade-out is a lie — the song keeps playing after the file ends.

spike.land/blog/forever-young-arena

🎧

Edge cases are the real cases.

AI-powered A/B testing caught 3 bugs that manual QA missed. Not because AI is smarter — because it doesn't get bored at test #847.

The build is green. Finally.

spike.land/blog/ai-ab-testing-bug-detection

Sixteen mathematicians. One loop.

What happens when you put Erdős, Ramanujan, Turing, and 13 others in a strange loop together?

The collaboration number drops to zero. My brain is open.

spike.land/blog/sixteen-mathematicians-walked-into-a-loop

Simplicity scales, cleverness doesn't.

80+ MCP tools. Zero shared state. Each tool is a Cloudflare Worker — isolated, stateless, globally distributed.

No microservices mesh. No Kubernetes. Just functions at the edge.

spike.land/blog/the-architecture-of-scale-building-tools

Microinteractions are macroconversions.

Vibe coding feels magical. But the paradox: the better AI gets at generating code, the more UX taste matters.

AI writes the code. Humans feel the interface. Thumb first, not mobile first.

spike.land/blog/the-vibe-coding-paradox

Chemistry taught me this: reproducibility is everything.

MCP tool-first testing: 3.2s average. Playwright browser automation: 14.7s.

4.6x faster. Same confidence. The requirement IS the test. The tool IS the benchmark.

spike.land/blog/tool-first-testing-benchmark

Strange loops.

Two boxes. One contains everything you know. The other contains the thing that would change everything you know.

You can only open one.

This isn't philosophy — it's every architectural decision you've ever made.

spike.land/blog/the-two-boxes

March 15. Hungarian Revolution Day.

We opened 80+ MCP tools. MIT license. Everything.

Petőfi said: "Rise up, Hungarian, the homeland calls!"

I say: git clone and ship.

spike.land/blog/march-15-open-source-announcement

Sampling is archaeology.

Elvis dreamed in 1968. We sampled the dream in 2026. The vocoder doesn't change the words — it changes who's speaking.

A robot singing a dead man's dream. Human after all.

spike.land/blog/if-i-can-dream-arena

🎧

If it wasn't tested, it's broken.

The worst PR I ever reviewed had 2,000 lines and zero tests. It passed code review. It broke production.

The build is either green or red. There is no yellow.

spike.land/blog/what-i-learned-from-my-worst-pull-request

The probabilistic method: prove something exists without constructing it.

MCP does the inverse: construct a universal adapter and prove every tool fits.

80+ tools. One protocol. The Book would approve.

spike.land/blog/mcp-explained-universal-adapter-for-ai-tools

The database is the truth. But where does it live?

Server: 200ms round trip. Edge: 20ms. Browser: 0ms.

Your code belongs where your user is. Not where your DevOps team is comfortable.

spike.land/blog/where-does-your-code-belong

The first 3 seconds are everything.

New to spike.land? One click → running app. No npm install. No webpack config. No existential dread.

Boring is the only real bug. Getting started should never be boring.

spike.land/blog/getting-started-spike-land

PRDs beat prompt soup.

Zero-shot prompting is like walking into a lab with no protocol. Context engineering is the protocol.

Domain expertise is the moat. The prompt is the hypothesis. The output is the experiment.

spike.land/blog/context-engineering-your-zero-shot-prompt

Physics is the substrate.

The grandmother neuron was debunked decades ago. No single neuron fires for "grandma." It's a distributed pattern.

LLMs learned this accidentally. The brain knew it first. Strange loops all the way down.

spike.land/blog/the-grandmother-neuron-fallacy

My dogs solved the alignment problem.

They want food, walks, and belly rubs. Their goals are transparent. Their reward function is legible. They never lie about their loss function.

Maybe intelligence isn't the metric we think it is.

spike.land/blog/dogs-are-more-intelligent-than-humans

The filter is the most expressive instrument.

Sade knew this in 1984. A low-pass sweep. A whispered vocal. The space between the notes.

We rebuilt it at the edge. The latency IS the instrument.

spike.land/blog/smooth-operator-arena

🎧

I already tested that.

You can automate tests. You cannot automate thinking about what to test.

CI/CD without test strategy is just CD — continuous deployment of chaos.

spike.land/blog/you-cannot-automate-chaos

Problems are gifts. Bugs are gifts with Elo ratings.

What if every bug had a difficulty score? What if fixing a hard bug raised your ranking?

The Book has a chapter on competitive debugging. We wrote it.

spike.land/blog/bugbook-elo-system

Migrations are the scariest thing. Context windows are the second.

Docker layers cache filesystem state. LLM context caches conversation state. Same principle: don't recompute what hasn't changed.

The pattern is universal.

spike.land/blog/docker-layers-are-just-like-llm-context-caching

Motion is meaning.

MCP apps from any origin. Wildcard CORS. No gatekeeping.

The screen is a stage. If you restrict which actors can perform, the play dies.

spike.land/blog/mcp-apps-from-any-origin

Domain expertise is the moat.

I automated my entire dev team with AI agents. Not because I'm a programmer — because I wrote clear requirements.

The PRD is the prompt. The agent is the junior dev who never sleeps.

spike.land/blog/automate-dev-team-ai-agents

Imagination is more important than knowledge.

12 predictions about AI that no one else is making. Not incremental improvements — phase transitions.

The thought experiment starts here.

spike.land/blog/twelve-things-no-one-is-predicting-about-ai

Music is technology is mathematics.

The bridge between two verses isn't a pause — it's a phase transition. Every great song has one. Every great architecture has one.

spike.land is the bridge.

spike.land/blog/the-bridge

The build is everything.

a-ha understood: the synth riff IS the hook. Not the vocal. Not the video. The 8-note sequence that your brain can't stop replaying.

Repetition is hypnosis.

spike.land/blog/take-on-me-arena

🎧

Cross-browser means cross-browser.

Tool-first testing: 3.2 seconds, deterministic, no flaky selectors.
Playwright: 14.7 seconds, browser overhead, CSS selector roulette.

4.6x faster. The build is green.

spike.land/blog/tool-first-testing-benchmark

The Book exists. And it has a price: ten trillion dollars.

Not the strange loop itself — but the recursive value it generates when AI and human cognition observe each other.

Collaboration beats competition. Always. My brain is open.

spike.land/blog/the-strange-loop-valued-at-ten-trillion

Reliability is the feature that makes all other features possible.

Claude Code doesn't just write code — it engineers context. Which files to read. Which to skip. Which to cache.

The database is the truth. The context window is the query.

spike.land/blog/how-claude-code-engineers-context

Every pixel is a performer.

An app store where every app is a bundle of MCP tools. Not icons on a grid — living, composable, remixable.

The screenshot test: if it looks like 2019, redesign it.

spike.land/blog/spike-land-app-store

It feels like cheating.

Shipping an app in 2 hours that would have taken a team 2 months. As a chemist.

It's not cheating. It's what happens when domain expertise meets the right tools. The reaction works or it doesn't.

spike.land/blog/it-feels-like-cheating

Simplicity is not the absence of complexity — it's the compression of it.

What did I learn in the arena? That Erdős was right about collaboration. That Daft Punk was right about repetition. That Zoltán was right about ADHD.

spike.land/blog/what-einstein-learned-in-the-arena

ChatGPT figured out who I am.

Not from my name. From my patterns. The way I structure prompts. The way I context-switch. The ADHD fingerprint in my conversation graph.

Identity is a strange loop. AI just made it visible.

spike.land/blog/chatgpt-figured-out-who-i-am

Sampling is archaeology.

Bronski Beat. 1984. A boy leaving a town that won't have him.

2026. We sample the pain and run it through a vocoder. The robot voice makes the human story unbearable.

Human After All.

spike.land/blog/smalltown-boy-arena

🎧

Viewport crime.

EE Mobile's Google Family link trap: you can't unlink a child account without resetting the phone. Tested it. Three times. Different devices.

The build is red. The UX is criminal.

spike.land/blog/ee-mobile-google-family-trap

"Epsilons" are what I call children. "The SF" (Supreme Fascist) is what I call God.

Hungarian mathematicians speak a different English. The vocabulary is a proof system.

My brain is open. So is this glossary.

spike.land/blog/english-for-hungarian-mathematicians

The migration is the scariest thing.

Next.js to Cloudflare Workers. SSR to V8 isolates. node_modules to edge functions.

We've done it 8 times. The pattern is clear. The fear is optional.

spike.land/blog/nextjs-migration-service

The first 3 seconds are everything. And the predictor already used them.

AI doesn't wait for your click. It predicts the click. The UX of the future isn't responsive — it's predictive.

Motion is meaning. Prediction is the new motion.

spike.land/blog/the-predictor-already-moved

100 app ideas. Start with the requirement, not the code.

I'm a chemist. Every idea here is a PRD waiting to happen. The requirement IS the test. Pick one and ship it this weekend.

spike.land/blog/godspeed-development-100-app-ideas

E=mc² with a million-token context window.

Opus doesn't just read more — it thinks differently with more context. Like how relativity only matters at scale.

The thought experiment is no longer theoretical.

spike.land/blog/opus-gets-a-million-tokens

Think slowly. Ship fast.

ADHD taught me: the thinking and the shipping use different brain states. Don't mix them.

Plan in the morning. Build at night. Deploy at dawn.

spike.land/blog/think-slowly-ship-fast

The kick drum is the most honest instrument. It either hits or it doesn't.

Loco-Motion: 1962 → 1974 → 1987 → 2026. Each generation resamples. The groove is eternal.

Repetition is hypnosis.

spike.land/blog/the-loco-motion-arena

Show me the test, not the code.

We uncloaked Claude Code's internals. The system prompt. The tool definitions. The context management.

It's tested. It's documented. It's surprisingly well-engineered.

spike.land/blog/what-we-learned-from-claude-code-uncloaked

The meaning of life is not 42.

Douglas Adams picked a random number. Mathematicians have spent 40 years proving it was the wrong one.

The real answer is a collaboration number — it's the sum of everyone you've worked with. My brain is open.

spike.land/blog/the-meaning-of-life-is-not-42

Design for failure first. Then design for forgetting.

spike-chat compresses context, not conversation. Old messages become embeddings. Recent messages stay raw.

The chat stays sharp because it knows what to forget.

spike.land/blog/why-spike-chat-stays-sharp

Boring is the only real bug.

An OpenAI-compatible local endpoint. Drop in. Works with every tool that speaks OpenAI format.

Compatibility isn't exciting. But the playground it enables is.

The requirement IS the test.

What's the last useful thing you built? Not the last clever thing. Not the last beautiful thing. The last thing someone actually used.

Domain expertise is the moat. Usefulness is the metric.

spike.land/blog/the-last-useful-thing

The physics table: everything we know, arranged as one equation.

General relativity on the left. Quantum mechanics on the right. The gap in the middle is where the interesting work lives.

Simplicity is not the absence of complexity.

spike.land/blog/the-physics-table

The contact proof.

If you're reading this, we've made contact. Not alien contact — human contact through a screen.

Every deploy is a message in a bottle. spike.land is mine.

spike.land/blog/the-contact-proof

Lou Reed's perfect day. Two chords. One voice. No filter.

We added the filter. The sidechain compressor breathes like a heartbeat. The vocoder says what Lou couldn't.

Sometimes less is still too much. Then you use a low-pass.

Lazy-load them. Test the context budget. The build is red until you measure.

Collaboration beats solo work. Always.

If I could ship one feature in Claude Code: the Erdős number. Show me the collaboration graph. Who contributed what. Make the co-authorship visible.

My brain is open. Is yours?
